comments.text: Descoped for Octopus (octo-h7b shipped bdd7536f): `ticket_show` with `recursive: true` now walks the subtree in Elixir and returns `{root, tickets, tree, deps, ready}` — `tree` and `deps` are graphviz DOT strings embedded in the JSON response, so agents read them directly. Still useful for `bw` CLI users; demoting to P3.

description:
Beadwork has subtree traversal primitives for `ready`/`blocked` (`buildSubtreeOverlay`, `buildSubtreeSet`, `LoadEdges` in `internal/issue/blocking.go`). These are not exposed via a generic show verb.

**Fix:** Add either `bw tree <id>` or `bw show <id> --recursive` that walks the subtree and renders:
- parent/child hierarchy as an indented tree
- blocked_by/blocks edges (DAG) as annotations on nodes
- optional `--json` for machine consumption (same shape that MCP wrappers would relay)

**Acceptance:**
- Epic with 3 levels of descendants renders as a readable tree.
- DAG mode shows external blocker arrows (a child blocked by a ticket outside the subtree).
- JSON output is enough for a downstream MCP tool to render the tree client-side in one call.
title: bw show --recursive / bw tree <id> — subtree/DAG rendering
